title: Kubernetes RBAC binding
description: One record per (Role|ClusterRole, RoleBinding|ClusterRoleBinding) grant in a Kubernetes cluster: which subjects (users / groups / service-accounts) hold which role, and the rules (apiGroups + resources + verbs) that role grants. Pulled read-only from the Kubernetes API (get/list on rbac.authorization.k8s.io roles/clusterroles/rolebindings/clusterrolebindings). RBAC configuration only — never Secret values, ConfigMap values, container env, or logs. A connector-side heuristic flags wildcard / cluster-admin-grade rules; the platform evaluator decides pass/fail per (control, binding).

binding_name.description: Name of the RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding.

binding_scope.description: Whether the binding is cluster-wide (ClusterRoleBinding) or namespaced (RoleBinding).

namespace.description: Namespace of a namespaced binding. Empty / absent for cluster-scoped bindings.

role_name.description: Name of the referenced Role or ClusterRole.

subjects.description: Subjects the binding grants the role to (identity references only — no credentials).

name.description: Subject name (identity reference, not a credential).

rules.description: The policy rules the referenced role grants (apiGroups + resources + verbs). Authorization configuration only.

api_groups.description: API groups the rule applies to ('' is the core group; '*' is all).

resources.description: Resource kinds the rule applies to ('*' is all).

verbs.description: Verbs the rule grants (get, list, create, '*', ...).

grants_wildcard.description: Connector-side heuristic: at least one rule grants a wildcard verb / resource / apiGroup (cluster-admin-grade reach).
